message:
fix(atc): broadcast deadlock advisories to all cycle participants, fix agent lookup normalization

Two issues in the ATC (Air Traffic Control) engine:

1. **Deadlock advisories only reached the "best target" agent.** When a
   reservation deadlock cycle was detected, the remediation logic sent
   an advisory message only to the single agent identified as the
   likeliest stale-holder. The other agents in the cycle had no idea
   they were part of a deadlock and would continue waiting indefinitely.
   Now the advisory loop iterates over ALL agents in the detected cycle,
   sending each one the same diagnostic message and recording an effect
   plan for each. This gives every participant visibility into the
   bottleneck and a chance to release stale reservations proactively.

2. **`atc_agent_last_activity` applied redundant `.to_ascii_lowercase()`.**
   Agent names in the ATC engine's `agents` HashMap are already stored
   in their canonical form (lowercased at insertion time). Calling
   `.to_ascii_lowercase()` on the lookup key was unnecessary and
   created a new String allocation on every call. Removed the
   normalization to use the agent name directly, matching the existing
   storage convention.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
